originalTitle: Shelter-in-place ordered in LA as blaze engulfs roof of 500,000 sq-ft warehouse
summary: Clouds of dark smoke and ammonia rose from cold storage and freezing facility in Boyle Heights near downtown
content: The solar panel-covered roof of a 500,000 sq-ft cold storage facility in Los Angeles caught fire Wednesday, sending up thick clouds of dark smoke and ammonia, leading city officials to order nearby residents to shelter in place. About 110 firefighters responded in the afternoon to a blaze at the cold storage and freezing warehouse owned by Lineage in the Boyle Heights neighborhood near downtown, according to spokesperson Jennifer Middleton of the Los Angeles fire department. Both the city and Los Angeles county deployed firefighters and hazmat teams to contain the fire. The cause of the fire remains under investigation, Middleton said. By the time firefighters arrived, the solar panels covering the roof had caught fire. “I urge everyone in the impacted area to get indoors immediately, close windows and doors, turn off air conditioning, and avoid unnecessary travel to the area,” Los Angeles mayor Karen Bass wrote in a tweet. Firefighters first tried to stop the fire by spraying it with hand lines on the roof, but were forced to retreat after an ammonia line burst, according to LAFD chief Jaime Moore. “At some point, an ammonia line was compromised and we had a large, pressurized off-gassing of ammonia,” Moore told NBC News 4. Cold storage facilities rely on ammonia as a cost-effective refrigerant, but it is both toxic and flammable. Moore warned residents with health conditions to avoid breathing the contaminated air by staying indoors with windows closed and air conditioning off while the city monitored the air for evidence of ammonia off-gassing. The fire grew so large and hazardous to approach that responders took the unusual step of dousing it from above with helicopters, while firefighters retreated from the roof. At least three helicopters delivered more than half a dozen air drops of water, according to Middleton. “The amount of fire that they had was overrunning the amount of water we were able to put on it and our firefighters had to evacuate the area for a few minutes there while it was off-gassing,” Moore said. Lineage did not immediately respond to a request for comment. The roof fire had been largely contained by the evening, Middleton said. But the roof showed signs of sagging and the presence of solar panels raised concerns the building may contain lithium ion batteries to store the energy gathered from the sun. Moore called lithium ion batteries “one of our newest challenges in the fire service”. Lithium ion batteries are capable of a process called “thermal runaway” that makes it difficult to extinguish them. “It’s extremely hard to fight the fire, it’s dangerous to be around the fire, and it’s dangerous to be around the extremely hazardous smoke,” Middleton said of lithium ion battery fires.
chineseTitle: 大火吞噬了 500,000 平方英尺仓库的屋顶，洛杉矶订购了就地避难所
chineseSummary: 市中心附近博伊尔高地的冷藏和冷冻设施升起黑烟和氨云
chineseContent: 洛杉矶一座 500,000 平方英尺的冷藏设施覆盖太阳能电池板的屋顶周三起火，冒出浓浓的黑烟和氨气，导致市政府官员命令附近居民就地避难。洛杉矶消防局发言人詹妮弗·米德尔顿 (Jennifer Middleton) 表示，下午，约 110 名消防员对位于市中心附近博伊尔高地 (Boyle Heights) 地区 Lineage 拥有的冷藏和冷冻仓库发生的火灾进行了救援。该市和洛杉矶县都部署了消防员和危险品小组来控制火势。米德尔顿说，起火原因仍在调查中。当消防队员到达时，覆盖屋顶的太阳能电池板已经着火。洛杉矶市长凯伦·巴斯在推文中写道：“我敦促受影响地区的每个人立即进入室内，关闭门窗，关闭空调，并避免不必要的前往该地区的旅行。”洛杉矶消防局局长海梅·摩尔 (Jaime Moore) 表示，消防队员首先试图用手绳向屋顶喷水来阻止火势，但在氨水管道破裂后被迫撤退。摩尔告诉 NBC 新闻 4：“在某个时候，氨管道受到了损害，我们产生了大量氨的加压废气。”冷藏设施依赖氨作为一种经济高效的制冷剂，但它有毒且易燃。摩尔警告有健康状况的居民，在城市监测空气中是否有氨气逸出的证据时，请呆在室内，关上窗户和空调，以避免呼吸受污染的空气。火势变得如此之大，接近起来非常危险，以至于救援人员采取了不同寻常的措施，用直升机从上方扑灭火势，而消防队员则从屋顶撤退。至少三架直升机交付了一半以上
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
